birth.historical_notes: Wurtemberg (Württemberg) was a German kingdom 1806-1918, now part of Baden-Württemberg state. The family emigrated from there to NY State.
Wurtemberg (Württemberg) was a German kingdom 1806-1918, now part of Baden-Württemberg state. The family emigrated from there to NY State.

death.note: Per EH 2437: d. Oct 5, 1907. Likely Brockport NY where the family resided.

marriages.notes: Per EH 3891: m. June 19, 1878 Alice Gordon, dau of James + Polly (Brewster) Gordon, b. Oct 21, 1852 Rushford NY. Resided Fairport NY. Two children: James Gordon Hooker (EH 5047, b.1881 m.1906 Ambia Boody Harris — son James Gordon Hooker Jr) and Emma Hooker (EH 5048, b.1880 m.1905 Richard L. Saunders).

bio.extra_names: Per Mt. Hope Cemetery + Gordon family records: also recorded as 'Ida Mae'

marriages.notes: Per EH 3892: m. Nov 18, 1873 George Cooley Gordon (b. July 1, 1849 Rushford NY, son of Luther + Florilla Cooley Gordon; d. Aug 25, 1898). FOUR sons per EH 5049-5052: Luther Gordon (b.1875, m.1904 Augusta Moore, 2 children Luther + Emeline), George Cooley Gordon (b.1877, m.1906 Cecelia Mitchell Kimball, dau Cecelia), Fred Hooker Gordon (b.1881, m.1906 Florence De Moss Robinson), Thomas Cullen Gordon (b.1884, m.1908 Ruth Little Perry). Two of her sons named for Hooker family: Fred HOOKER Gordon and Thomas CULLEN Gordon — `named_for_hooker` pattern applies to those two.

narrative_blocks.header: Brockport's banker matriarch served Rochester children
narrative_blocks.body: Ida May Hooker — born January 1855 in Kendall NY, daughter of Thomas Cullen Hooker — moved to Brockport NY when her father relocated the family there. She married George Cooley Gordon Sr. on October 18, 1875 when she was 20. George went on to become president of the First National Bank of Brockport, a 'millionaire banker' per his New York Times obituary.

narrative_blocks.header: Gordon Hall and Tiffany window honor her
narrative_blocks.body: Ida and George's enduring presence in Brockport survived her death by decades. In 1927, a Tiffany window made by Louis C. Tiffany was installed at St. Luke's Episcopal Church in Brockport, dedicated to George and Ida's memory.

narrative_blocks.header: Among first women on SUNY Brockport Board, 1917
narrative_blocks.body: In 1917 Ida May Hooker Gordon was appointed to the Board of Trustees of SUNY Brockport, one of the first two women to serve on the board. The appointment paralleled her presidency of the Rochester Children's Nursery — extending her institutional leadership beyond philanthropy into educational governance. She continued to serve into her later years before her death at 91.

burial.plot_notes: Mt. Hope Cemetery, Rochester NY — Lot 61 Range 2 — purchased by her father-in-law Luther Gordon Sr. Plot features a stunning monument with columns and an allegorical figure with drapery. George C. Gordon her husband paid $9,229.54 for perpetual care in 1883.

death.cause: Hemorrhage of the stomach. Died at his summer residence at Troutburg on Lake Ontario.

burial.plot_notes: Mt. Hope Cemetery, Rochester NY — Lot 61 Range 2 (the Gordon family plot purchased by his father Luther Sr.). Predeceased his wife Ida by ~48 years.

parents.father_research_notes: Luther Gordon Sr. (1818-1881, Brockport bank founder, congressional nominee 1872, lumber merchant)

careers.notes: Succeeded his father Luther Gordon Sr. as bank president 1881; held position until his 1898 death.
With father Luther; family had 7,000+ acres of timberland in Michigan.

sources.page: 'George C. Gordon, the millionaire banker of Brockport, died yesterday at his summer Residence at Troutburg, on the shore of Lake Ontario.'

sources.page: Reports the 1863 'Bold Bank Robbery' incident: at age 15, working at Waters, Bishop & Co. banking office, George was 'felled by a blow upon the head, dragged into the bank vault and the lock turned upon him' during a $5,000 robbery.

notable.primary_url_label: NYT obituary August 26, 1898 (no current online URL)

narrative_blocks.body: George C. Gordon was already at work in his father's banking world when, at age 15 on July 23, 1863, he was attending the banking office of Waters, Bishop & Co. A man asked him to change a ten-dollar bill. Per the Daily Union and Advertiser: 'he was felled by a blow upon the head, dragged into the bank vault and the lock turned upon him.' He was found 'severely beaten upon the head, and was bleeding from a wound,' and $5,000 had been stolen.

narrative_blocks.header: Succeeded father as Brockport bank president
narrative_blocks.body: Upon his father Luther's 1881 death, George C. Gordon stepped into the presidency of the First National Bank of Brockport — the bank his father had founded as the repository for Erie Canal tolls collected at Brockport. He held that position until his own 1898 death.

bio.extra_names: Named after his grandfather Luther Gordon Sr. (1818-1881)

sources.page: Luther Gordon Jr.: enrolled UR class of 1899; withdrew 1898 upon father's death to manage family bank. Member UR Glee and Mandolin Clubs (played guitar).

Edward Hooker 1909 (entry 5049 — birth date matches July 1875)

narrative_blocks.header: Withdrew from Rochester to run father's bank
narrative_blocks.body: When his father died in 1898, Luther Gordon Jr. was 23 and a junior at the University of Rochester, set to graduate in 1899 alongside his brother George Cooley Gordon II. Both brothers withdrew that year to take over the family's Brockport businesses. Luther became President of the First National Bank of Brockport (1898-1921), succeeding his father; George II became Assistant Cashier and later VP and Treasurer.

narrative_blocks.header: A Tiffany window for his parents
narrative_blocks.body: As treasurer of St. Luke’s Episcopal Church he likely oversaw the 1927 installation of its Tiffany window, dedicated to his parents George Gordon and Ida Hooker. He never married, and died of a tumor at forty-nine.

death.note: Died in infancy at approximately 6 months old. Per Mt. Hope Cemetery records, his stone is prominently placed in the Gordon family plot, directly below the angel monument — an inscription affectionately calls him "Willie."

burial.plot_notes: Mt. Hope Cemetery, Rochester NY — Lot 61 Range 2 (Gordon family plot). His stone is set apart from the rest of the family stones (which follow a square perimeter), prominently placed below the central angel monument. Inscribed 'Willie.'

narrative_blocks.header: He quit the family bank to farm
narrative_blocks.body: At twenty-nine he walked away from the Gordon family bank for his own ground — Whitehall Farms, north of Brockport. His son Bob spent seventy years giving to a Rochester college that put the family name on its field house.

burial.plot_notes: Mount Hope Cemetery, Rochester NY — Section MM Lot 266

marriages.notes: Per EH 5052 + family records: m. Oct 7, 1908 Ruth Little Perry (b. Sep 1883 Illinois USA, d. after 1930). Two sons documented in Sam's article: Thomas C. Jr. and William Ward Gordon.

education.notes: Studied at Yale for 3 years per Clarke 1908. Did not complete degree.

careers.notes: Succeeded brother Luther Jr as bank president after Luther's 1921 death. Replaced the 1873 bank building with the present Neo-classical structure (now Chase Manhattan Bank).
Spent 10 years as chairman per Clarke 1908.

careers.notes: Greenhouses started as hobby; became business. Now Roger's Flower Shop and Greenhouses per Andrews 1908.

notable.primary_url_label: Clarke 1908 + Andrews family history (no current online URL)

narrative_blocks.header: Yale 3 years, then Brockport mayor and banker
narrative_blocks.body: Thomas Cullen Gordon — named for his maternal grandfather Thomas Cullen Hooker — studied at Yale University for three years before returning to Brockport without completing his degree. He served as Trustee of the Village of Brockport (1914-1920), Chairman of the Board of Water Commissions (1915-1920), and Mayor of Brockport (1920-1922). After his elder brother Luther Gordon Jr.'s 1921 death, he took over as President of the First National Bank of Brockport — replacing the 1873 building with a Neo-classical structure that today houses Chase Manhattan Bank.

narrative_blocks.header: His brother’s death made him president
narrative_blocks.body: When Luther Gordon Jr. died in 1921, Thomas took over the First National Bank of Brockport and replaced its 1873 building with a Neo-classical structure that still stands today as a Chase Manhattan branch. He was treasurer of St. Luke’s Episcopal — the same parish his brother had served as treasurer before him.

burial.plot_notes: Mount Hope Cemetery, Rochester NY (Gordon family plot Lot 61 Range 2)

birth.historical_notes: Hindsburg / Hindsburgh is a hamlet in the Town of Murray, Orleans County NY. Born 11 years after sister Ida May.
